Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets first parse the log lines Each line contains timestamp action type and additional attributes separated by commas The attributes are key value pairs like App Window and some have Keys Action Direction Text

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Cases should represent a logical unit of work for example editing a specific document handling a particular email reviewing a certain PDF or working on an Excel file

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: From the log we see activities involving different documents and files Document1 docx appears early and then again later Quarterly Report docx appears at the beginning and again near the end Email activity forms a reply sequence Report Draft pdf has review actions and Budget 2024 xlsx has editing actions

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user sometimes returns to the same document later for example Document1 docx appears early and then after Excel they return That suggests the same case editing Document1 docx spans multiple intervals So case should be based on the document or entity being worked on

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For instance Document1 docx editing could be a case Then later Quarterly Report docx editing might be another case Also email interaction might be a separate case like responding to an email about Annual Meeting The PDF review of Report Draft pdf might be a case The Excel budget file is a case

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For email the window is Email Inbox but the actions indicate a specific email thread opening replying and sending This seems like a process opening reading replying sending So we can treat it as a case and derive the subject Annual Meeting

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For events that clearly belong to a specific document or window we assign the case based on that document or window For Microsoft Word documents use the filename For Excel use the filename For PDF use the filename For email use a derived identifier from the subject Annual Meeting

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Switch events are meta activities not directly part of the business task They are about changing context rather than doing work within a case So they may be excluded from the event log if the goal is meaningful process activities

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If we include Switch events we need to decide whether they belong to the source case or the destination case That becomes messy because a single event would relate to two contexts

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Better approach Use only events that are directly tied to a single window or application Focus Typing Save Close Click Scroll Highlight Switch events are about transitioning and do not fit a single case So we can exclude Switch events

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Map the raw actions to more abstract activities that represent user goals Focus to Open Document or Access Document Typing in Word or Excel to Edit Document Typing in Chrome email to Compose Email Reply Save to Save Document Close to Close Document Click Open Email to Open Email Click Reply to Email to Reply to Email Click Send Email to Send Email Scroll in email to Read Email Scroll in PDF to Review PDF Highlight in PDF to Highlight Text

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Document1 docx then has Open Document Edit Document Edit Document Save Document later Edit Document Save Document Close Document Quarterly Report docx has Open Document later Open Document Edit Document Save Document Close Document Budget 2024 xlsx has Open Document Edit Document Edit Document Save Document Email Annual Meeting has Open Email Read Email Reply to Email Compose Email Reply Send Email Report Draft pdf has Review PDF and Highlight Text

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The final ordered event list is built chronologically with case ids Document1 docx Quarterly Report docx Budget 2024 xlsx Report Draft pdf and Email Annual Meeting and each kept event receives a higher level activity name
